active_alerts.description:
Affected stops:
Harvard Sq @ Garden St - Dawes Island (inbound)
Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Affected stops:
Massachusetts Ave @ Johnston Gate (outbound)
Harvard Sq @ Garden St - Dawes Island (outbound)
Harvard Sq @ Garden St - Dawes Island (inbound)

To exit, exit the train at Copley, then cross Boylston St to the eastbound entrance. Proceed to the eastbound platform, then take an eastbound train to Arlington and use Arlington Elevator 963. See station personnel at the Copley eastbound lobby to gain entry at the faregate. To board, use Arlington Elevator 963 to access the eastbound platform. Take an eastbound train to Park Street, then proceed to the westbound platform and transfer to a westbound train.

Affected stops:
Harvard Sq @ Garden St - Dawes Island (inbound)
Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Affected routes:
426
429
435
441
442
455

To exit, South Station Elevator 949 located on the opposite side of the lobby. Alternatively, travel up the ramp towards the Commuter Rail Concourse, and at the base of the escalator, turn left and proceed down the hallway. At the end of the hallway, use South Station Elevator 6476 to access the Commuter Rail Concourse. To board, cross Atlantic Ave towards Dewey Sq and use South Station Elevator 949.

Service to Medford/Tufts:
Riders traveling to Medford/Tufts will need to transfer to an E Branch train at Government Center.

Alternate Service:
Riders can use Route 39 bus between Copley and Heath Street, which will be enhanced with more frequent service. 

Travel Time:
Riders using the Route 39 should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to Government Center from Heath Street should budget at least an additional 10 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute.

Affected stations:
Government Center
Park Street
Boylston
Arlington
Copley
Prudential
Symphony
Northeastern University
Museum of Fine Arts
Longwood Medical Area
Brigham Circle
Fenwood Road
Mission Park
Riverway
Back of the Hill
Heath Street

There are accessible mini-high platforms at the Western end of both the Track 1 and Track 2 sides. However, the existing pedestrian path between the two sides is long and includes steep slopes, especially on the Crest Road bridge over the tracks. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

Affected direction: westbound

Affected stations:
Englewood Avenue
Scheduled to complete sometime in 2027, the Reconstruction of Foster Street includes widening and resurfacing with the addition of bicycle lanes.

Skipped outbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

Skipped inbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

Skipped inbound stops:
Hammond St @ Plympton St

Affected routes:
64
70

Affected lines:
Foxboro Event Service
Providence/Stoughton Line

Affected routes:
60
65
Please use Back Bay Elevator 855 (Commuter Rail Track 1 & 3)

Pedestrian Access:
There is no access between the Hingham Ferry Terminal and DCR building. Pedestrian access to and from the ferry dock is through and around the west side of the Hingham Ferry Terminal. Some sidewalk access at the Terminal may also be restricted during this work.

Shipyard Drive:
Shipyard Drive is closed to traffic in front of the Hingham Ferry Terminal. Traffic is being rerouted through the Hingham Parking lot. Drop-offs and pick-ups have been relocated to the parking lot. Traffic police and signage will be in place to direct traffic.

Skipped outbound stops:
Harvard

Skipped inbound stops:
Harvard
Please use nearby ramp to the platform.

Affected lines:
Fall River/New Bedford Line

Tappan Street will remain closed in the outbound direction only (toward Cleveland Circle) from June 13 through July 10.

Affected stations:
Tappan Street

Accessibility:
Free and accessible shuttle buses are operating between Broadway & Quincy Center and between Broadway & Ashmont. Accessible van service is also available. Riders should see station personnel for assistance.

Alternate service:
Riders are encouraged to consider Commuter Rail service between South Station and Braintree, which will be free during this service change. Regular fares should be purchased for travel beyond Braintree.

Travel times:
Riders using shuttle bus service should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider traveling to Park Street from Ashmont/Braintree should budget at least 40 – 50 minutes of travel time in addition to their regular commute.

Shuttle Locations:

Broadway:
W. Broadway @ Broadway Station

Andrew:
Northbound - Dorchester Ave @ Leeds St
Southbound - Dorchester Ave @ Dexter St

JFK/UMass:
Northbound - Inner Busway
Southbound - Outer Busway

North Quincy:
All shuttles - North Quincy Busway

Wollaston:
All shuttles - Newport Ave @ Brook St

Quincy Center:
Burgin Pkwy @ Quincy Ctr Station

Savin Hill:
Northbound - Dorchester Ave @ Savin Hill Ave
Southbound - Dorchester Ave @ Thornley Rd

Fields Corner:
Northbound - Dorchester Ave opp Charles St
Southbound - Dorchester Ave @ Fields Corner Station

Shawmut:
All shuttles - Dorchester Ave @ Centre St

Ashmont:
Ashmont Busway (Berths # 5 & 6)

Skipped outbound stops:
Oxford St @ Washington St
Oxford St @ Market St

The escalator will remain out of service for the foreseeable future. This decision was made after a thorough inspection determined that corrosion on the escalator’s steel truss was so severe that it could not be repaired further and that a complete replacement of the escalator would be required. Replacing the escalator at this location requires a more extended unit meeting modern escalator safety requirements. This longer unit will require significant alteration to the station entrance to meet code requirements. The MBTA has currently begun design work to understand what station upgrades will be needed.

Due to these complexities, we expect the escalator will remain out of service for a significant time. We recognize the challenges this poses to our riders, and please know we are working to identify a solution as quickly as possible. In the meantime, Government Center Station, which is fully accessible and serves the Blue Line, is under a quarter-mile from Bowdoin Station.
